name: Nate McMillan
distinctive_detail: Spent his whole playing career with one franchise as a defense-and-assists role guard who barely scored, then built a far longer second career as a respected head coach.
work_style: Defended, distributed, and did the unselfish little things for one team, then taught that same selfless game for decades.
2k_label: The Role Guard Who Became The Coach
comp_tone: Steady. For the person whose unselfish role-playing became the foundation for leading others.

tags.hook: The comp for a career where the playing days were essentially a decades-long apprenticeship — the real impact came from teaching the same selfless game from the bench.

name: Charlie Ward
distinctive_detail: Won the Heisman Trophy as a college quarterback and chose pro basketball instead, building a long career as a steady backup point guard in a sport he was less famous in.
work_style: Ran the second unit with calm, unselfish control, having walked away from the sport that made his name.
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the Heisman-QB-who-chose-basketball arc; the cross-sport story is the hook, verify if quoting.

tags.hook: The comp for someone who walked away from their highest-ceiling sport to quietly excel in a second one, building a career on terms nobody else would have chosen.

name: Reggie Evans
distinctive_detail: Went undrafted and could barely score at all, yet carved out a decade-plus career and became one of the league's best pure rebounders by being elite at exactly one unglamorous thing.
work_style: Chased every rebound and out-toughed everyone in the paint, offering nothing on offense and not needing to.
comp_tone: Blue-collar. For the person who can't do the flashy part at all but is genuinely elite at the dirty work.

tags.hook: The comp for a career built on exactly one thing the player did at an elite level and everything else is zero — undrafted, unscorable, undeniable.

name: Chris Bosh
distinctive_detail: Was the franchise centerpiece of his own team who left to become the sacrificial third star on a superteam, willingly shrinking his game and his numbers to win two titles, before blood clots forced him out of the game.
work_style: Gave up being the first option to do whatever the partnership needed, spacing the floor and defending, the skilled big who put winning over his own numbers.
career_traits: sacrificial third star on a superteam
2k_label: The Star Who Chose To Be Third
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the franchise-star-who-left-to-sacrifice-and-win arc; his career-ending blood clots are health and off the board, keep them out of any bite.

tags.hook: The comp for a franchise star who willingly unmade his own stats to become the sacrificial third piece, earning rings but losing the individual legacy in the trade.

name: Kevin Love
distinctive_detail: Was a 20-and-10 alpha putting up huge numbers on losing teams, then was traded to a superteam where he reinvented himself as a floor-spacing role player and finally won, and became a prominent voice on mental health.
work_style: Rebounded and stretched the floor, transforming from the first option on a loser into a complementary piece on a champion.
career_traits: reinvented as a role player to win
2k_label: The Alpha Who Became A Piece
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the stat-alpha-to-role-player-who-won arc; frame the role change as growth, not demotion.

tags.hook: The comp for a first-option alpha who had to dissolve into a supporting role to win, and whose legacy lives in the gap between the numbers he put up and the titles he chased.

name: DeMarcus Cousins
distinctive_detail: Was a dominant, skilled big man whose prime was wasted on a dysfunctional organization that never made the playoffs around him, and just as he reached a contender, a torn Achilles and a run of injuries took the rest.
work_style: Put up huge two-way numbers as the centerpiece, carrying franchises that never built a real team around him, then fought his body once he finally reached a winner.
career_traits: prime wasted on a dysfunctional org
derailed by injury just as it turned
comp_tone: [QA] Frame on the talent, the org failure, and the injury bad luck; do NOT roast the Achilles, aim any bite only at controllable patterns.

tags.hook: The comp for a dominant prime swallowed by the wrong situation, then swallowed again by injury just as the situation finally changed — peak and plateau both stolen.

name: Lamar Odom
distinctive_detail: Was a versatile do-everything forward who could handle, pass, rebound, and defend like a guard in a big man's body, winning Sixth Man of the Year and two titles by filling whatever gap the team had that night.
work_style: Did a little of everything and slotted into any role, starter or sixth man, the positionless glue a champion needed.
career_traits: guard skills in a big's body
filled whatever gap the team had
comp_tone: [QA] Confident on the versatile do-everything sixth-man identity; his off-court struggles are off the board, keep any bite on basketball patterns.

tags.hook: The comp for a career defined entirely by positional versatility and team-contextual value — the player who had no fixed role because he could fill every role, and champions kept needing exactly that.
